stem: The fetch-decode-execute cycle uses several special-purpose registers inside the CPU.
review_notes: 2024 J277/01 Q6(b) adapted from a free-text table into a matching widget to give instant feedback.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Match each register to the purpose it performs during the fetch-execute cycle.

right: Stores the address of the next instruction to be fetched
Holds the address in memory that is currently being read from or written to
Holds the data or instruction that has just been fetched from memory
Stores the result of calculations performed by the ALU
Decodes the opcode and operand of the current instruction

mark_points.text: PC — stores the address of the next instruction
MAR — holds the memory address to read from / write to
MDR — holds the data or instruction fetched from memory
ACC — stores the result of ALU calculations

misconceptions.label: MAR vs MDR swap
misconceptions.description: MAR holds the address; MDR holds the data at that address. Candidates commonly swap these.
